plan.original_query: What controls apply to API security?

steps.search_query: API security: authentication, authorization, rate limiting, input validation, and secure communication for APIs
steps.purpose: Find controls that require authentication/authorization for services, secure transport and encryption, input validation and sanitization, rate limiting/DoS protections, and API-specific access controls such as tokens and session management.

steps.search_query: API management and service acquisition: secure development, contract requirements, and supplier controls for APIs
steps.purpose: Find controls addressing secure design/development/testing of APIs, supply‑chain or acquisition clauses for third‑party APIs and services, and secure development lifecycle requirements.

step.search_query: API security: authentication, authorization, rate limiting, input validation, and secure communication for APIs
step.purpose: Find controls that require authentication/authorization for services, secure transport and encryption, input validation and sanitization, rate limiting/DoS protections, and API-specific access controls such as tokens and session management.

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
documents.description: Employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity (NPE) identities, attributes, and access rights supporting authentication and authorization decisions in accordance with [Assignment: policy] using [Assignment: mechanisms].

documents.title: Public Key-based Authentication
documents.description:
(a) For public key-based authentication:
  (1) Enforce authorized access to the corresponding private key; and
  (2) Map the authenticated identity to the account of the individual or group; and
(b) When public key infrastructure (PKI) is used:
  (1) Validate certificates by constructing and verifying a certification path to an accepted trust anchor, including checking certificate status information; and
  (2) Implement a local cache of revocation data to support path discovery and validation.

documents.title: Limits on Authorized Use
documents.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:
  (a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or
  (b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

documents.title: Privileged Commands and Access
documents.description:
(a) Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [Assignment: organization-defined needs] ; and
(b) Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

step.search_query: API management and service acquisition: secure development, contract requirements, and supplier controls for APIs
step.purpose: Find controls addressing secure design/development/testing of APIs, supply‑chain or acquisition clauses for third‑party APIs and services, and secure development lifecycle requirements.

documents.title: Acquisition Process
Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
documents.description:
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to produce a design specification and security and privacy architecture that:
  a. Is consistent with the organization’s security and privacy architecture that is an integral part the organization’s enterprise architecture;
  b. Accurately and completely describes the required security and privacy functionality, and the allocation of controls among physical and logical components; and
  c. Expresses how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together to provide required security and privacy capabilities and a unified approach to protection.

documents.title: Establish and Maintain Trust Relationship with Providers
documents.description: Establish, document, and maintain trust relationships with external service providers based on the following requirements, properties, factors, or conditions: [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy requirements, properties, factors, or conditions defining acceptable trust relationships].

documents.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
Data Ownership
documents.description:
(a) Include organizational data ownership requirements in the acquisition contract; and
(b) Require all data to be removed from the contractor’s system and returned to the organization within [Assignment: time frame].

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Acquisition Process
Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
documents.description: Employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity (NPE) identities, attributes, and access rights supporting authentication and authorization decisions in accordance with [Assignment: policy] using [Assignment: mechanisms].

documents.title: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
documents.description:
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to produce a design specification and security and privacy architecture that:
  a. Is consistent with the organization’s security and privacy architecture that is an integral part the organization’s enterprise architecture;
  b. Accurately and completely describes the required security and privacy functionality, and the allocation of controls among physical and logical components; and
  c. Expresses how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together to provide required security and privacy capabilities and a unified approach to protection.

documents.title: Establish and Maintain Trust Relationship with Providers
documents.description: Establish, document, and maintain trust relationships with external service providers based on the following requirements, properties, factors, or conditions: [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy requirements, properties, factors, or conditions defining acceptable trust relationships].

documents.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
Public Key-based Authentication
documents.description:
(a) For public key-based authentication:
  (1) Enforce authorized access to the corresponding private key; and
  (2) Map the authenticated identity to the account of the individual or group; and
(b) When public key infrastructure (PKI) is used:
  (1) Validate certificates by constructing and verifying a certification path to an accepted trust anchor, including checking certificate status information; and
  (2) Implement a local cache of revocation data to support path discovery and validation.

documents.title: Limits on Authorized Use
documents.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:
  (a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or
  (b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

documents.title: Data Ownership
documents.description:
(a) Include organizational data ownership requirements in the acquisition contract; and
(b) Require all data to be removed from the contractor’s system and returned to the organization within [Assignment: time frame].

documents.title: Privileged Commands and Access
documents.description:
(a) Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [Assignment: organization-defined needs] ; and
(b) Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

response.answer:
Applicable controls for API security from the supplied set are the following (with brief explanation of how each applies):

- [ia-9] Service Identification and Authentication — APIs and the services/apps that implement them must uniquely identify and authenticate before establishing communications. This addresses authenticating API clients, services, and backend components prior to API calls [ia-9].

- [ia-13] Identity Providers and Authorization Servers — Use identity providers/authorization servers to manage identities, attributes, and access rights that support authentication and authorization decisions for API consumers (users, devices, NPEs) per organizational policy and mechanisms (e.g., OAuth/OIDC deployments) [ia-13].

- [ia-5.2] Public Key-based Authentication — When APIs use public-key mechanisms (mutual TLS, client certificates), enforce protection of private keys, map authenticated identities to accounts, validate PKI certificate paths and statuses, and cache revocation data as needed for API authentication [ia-5.2].

- [ac-20.1] Limits on Authorized Use — Permit external systems or third-party integrations to access organization APIs only after verifying that the external system implements required controls or after retaining approved connection/processing agreements, covering third‑party API consumers and federated integrations [ac-20.1].

- [ac-17.4] Privileged Commands and Access — Restrict and authorize execution of privileged API operations or remote privileged access; require formats that provide assessable evidence and document rationale in the system security plan (applies to privileged API endpoints and admin interfaces) [ac-17.4].

- [sa-4] Acquisition Process — Ensure API-related acquisitions (APIs, API gateways, cloud services) include security/privacy requirements, strength and assurance requirements, controls, documentation, responsibility allocation, and acceptance criteria in contracts [sa-4].

- [sa-4.12] Data Ownership — In contracts for API-hosting or API-managed services, include data ownership and require removal/return of organizational data on contract termination, which applies to APIs that store/process organizational data with contractors [sa-4.12].

- [sa-17] Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design — Require API developers to produce design/specifications and security architecture that align with enterprise architecture and clearly describe security functionality and control allocation across API components [sa-17].

- [sa-15] Development Process, Standards, and Tools — Require API developers to follow a documented secure development process that explicitly addresses security/privacy requirements, specifies standards/tools/configurations, manages changes to those tools/processes, and periodically review them against organization-defined security/privacy requirements [sa-15].

- [sa-9.3] Establish and Maintain Trust Relationship with Providers — For externally provided API services or platform providers (API gateways, cloud APIs), establish, document, and maintain trust relationships based on organization-defined security/privacy requirements and conditions [sa-9.3].

If you need, I can map these controls to specific API controls (authentication, authorization, transport protection, client credential management, onboarding/contract clauses, developer requirements) using only the controls above.

plan.original_query: What policies relate to logging and monitoring?

steps.purpose: Find controls that establish requirements for event logging, log management, centralized collection, monitoring, alerting, and audit review across systems

step.purpose: Find controls that establish requirements for event logging, log management, centralized collection, monitoring, alerting, and audit review across systems

documents.title: Monitoring Physical Access
Automated Enforcement and Monitoring
documents.description: Enforce and monitor compliance with software installation policies using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

documents.title: Event Logging
Nonlocal Maintenance
Reference Monitor
documents.description: Implement a reference monitor for [Assignment: access control policies] that is tamperproof, always invoked, and small enough to be subject to analysis and testing, the completeness of which can be assured.

documents.title: Monitoring Physical Access
Automated Enforcement and Monitoring
documents.description: Enforce and monitor compliance with software installation policies using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

documents.title: Event Logging
Nonlocal Maintenance
Reference Monitor
documents.description: Implement a reference monitor for [Assignment: access control policies] that is tamperproof, always invoked, and small enough to be subject to analysis and testing, the completeness of which can be assured.

response.answer:
The supplied controls that relate to logging and monitoring are:

- [pe-6] Monitoring Physical Access — requires monitoring physical access, reviewing physical access logs at an organization-defined frequency and on specified events, and coordinating review results with incident response [pe-6].

- [au-2] Event Logging — defines identification of loggable event types, coordination with other entities for selection criteria, specification of which event types are logged and when, rationale for adequacy of selected event types for after‑the‑fact investigations, and periodic review/update of logged event types [au-2].

- [cm-11.3] Automated Enforcement and Monitoring — requires enforcing and monitoring compliance with software installation policies via organization-defined automated mechanisms (this is a form of monitoring of configuration/software state) [cm-11.3].

- [ma-4] Nonlocal Maintenance — requires approving and monitoring nonlocal maintenance activities and maintaining records for those activities (recordkeeping and monitoring of remote maintenance sessions) [ma-4].

Note: [ac-25] Reference Monitor is about enforcing access control policy via a tamperproof monitor; it is not itself a logging or monitoring control and does not mandate logging/monitoring activities, so it is not listed as a logging/monitoring policy here [ac-25].

plan.original_query: What is the capital of France?

response.answer: I could not find any NIST SP 800-53 control relevant to that question in the policy library, so I cannot answer it. Try rephrasing the question in terms of a security control, a control family, or a control ID such as AC-2.
